headline: Bills Officially Exclude OJ Simpson From New Stadium
writeup: The dominant story out of Buffalo on June 27 is the Bills' official decision to exclude O.J. Simpson from the Wall of Fame at the new Highmark Stadium, with the organization stating he is 'not a fit to display' as they move into a new era. The decision has generated widespread national reaction, with fans and media weighing in on how the franchise handles the complicated legacy of its all-time rushing leader. On the field, ESPN's ranking of the Bills' offensive playmaker group near the bottom of the NFL drew attention, though Josh Allen expressed confidence in new receiver DJ Moore, calling him a 'huge help.' Allen also praised new quarterbacks coach Bo Hardegree, while the team continues to evaluate potential trade targets to bolster the roster before the 2026 season. Meanwhile, rookie scouting reports and scheme-fit questions surrounding new defensive coordinator Jim Leonhard's system round out a busy offseason news cycle.
